# Example name

## Objective

State the single learning outcome and the problem the example helps compare.

## Maturity level

Choose and link one: Level 1 focused pattern, Level 2 layered example, or Level 3 architecture reference. Explain why.

## What it demonstrates

- Technology, protocol, or architecture concern.
- Important implementation boundary.
- Behavior worth observing.

## What it intentionally omits

List omitted security, resilience, data, testing, deployment, or operational concerns. Omissions are part of the example contract.

## Architecture

Describe only the example’s actual structure. Include a small Mermaid diagram when relationships are otherwise hard to follow.

## Request or message flow

Describe the happy path and important failure path. Use a sequence diagram for multi-component flows.

## Project structure

Show the meaningful folders/projects, not generated files.

## Configuration

List settings, environment variables, safe placeholders, ports, certificates, databases, and external services. Never include a live secret.

## How to run

Provide commands from the repository root and identify the expected endpoint or client.

## Test scenarios

List automated test commands and/or reproducible manual scenarios, including at least one failure case.

## Production considerations

Explain hardening, scale, availability, security, observability, data lifecycle, and compatibility work relevant to this example. Do not call an example production-ready.

## Related examples

Link the nearest comparisons and state what changes between them.

## Related standards

Link authoritative protocol/framework standards or repository ADRs that directly apply.
